title: Linux Advanced Administration
description: Learn advanced Linux commands for process management, file permissions, ownership modification, network testing, and logs inspection.

tasks.prompt: View all running system processes in detail.
tasks.explanation: 'ps aux' lists all running processes. 'top' or 'htop' displays them in an interactive real-time screen.

tasks.prompt: Forcefully terminate a process with Process ID (PID) '1234'.
tasks.explanation: The '-9' (SIGKILL) signal forces the operating system to immediately terminate the process.

tasks.prompt: Change permissions of 'script.sh' to owner: read/write/execute, others: read/execute (octal format).
tasks.hint: Use chmod command with octal 755: 'chmod 755 script.sh'
tasks.explanation: 'chmod 755' assigns full permissions (7) to the owner, and read/execute (5) to the group and others.

tasks.prompt: Change the owner and group of 'file.txt' to 'root' (both owner and group).
tasks.explanation: 'chown' (Change Owner) changes file user and group assignments. The syntax is owner:group.

tasks.prompt: Send exactly 4 ping requests to test connectivity to host '8.8.8.8'.
tasks.explanation: 'ping' checks system network accessibility. '-c 4' limits the ping count so it doesn't run infinitely.

tasks.prompt: View systemd journal logs in real-time (follow log output).
tasks.explanation: 'journalctl -f' displays the system logs as they generate in real time.
